label: Nasty Neve v3 — Transformer-Colored
_doc: Adds De Paiva 2011 transformer saturation post-mix. Transformer colors
EVERYTHING that goes through, even when MIX=0 (dry signal still gets
transformer character). That's the 'console always-on' sound real Neve
33609 hardware produces — the I/O transformers are part of the signal
chain whether the GR element is doing anything or not.
TOPOLOGY (9 user knobs):
in ─► n_in ─┬─► n_det ─► n_env ─► n_comp ─► n_vca.gainMod (sidechain)
└─► n_mix.dry
n_mix ─► n_xfm (xformerSat) ─► n_out ─► out
USER KNOBS:  IN  ATTACK  RELEASE  THRESHOLD  RATIO  KNEE  MIX  HEAT  OUT
HEAT = transformer drive (xformerSat.drive in dB).
0  dB = transformer barely engaged (clean-ish, slight 2H sheen)
6  dB = noticeable warmth, soft compression curvature
12 dB = clearly saturated, vintage console feel
18+   = aggressive, audible THD, can get tape-fuzz character
LOCKED params:
- n_det.mode             = peak    (transient response)
- n_env.amount/offset    = 1 / 0   (positive linear → gainComputer)
- n_vca.gainDb           = 0       (internal VCA stage)
- n_xfm.coreSize         = 1       (De Paiva 'a' nonlinear-cap exponent)
- n_xfm.sourceZ          = 600 Ω   (typical line-level Z)
- n_xfm.loss             = 0.3     (De Paiva 'b' hysteresis exponent)
- n_xfm.air              = 1       (HF corner at default; not exposed)
User can ride HEAT (drive) only; the other transformer params are
vetted by recipe author per De Paiva 2011 § Eqs 15+17 + Whitlock 2002
ch. on audio transformer tone.

ui._doc_lockedParams: Internal sidechain wiring + VCA stage + the transformer's deep DSP params (coreSize, sourceZ, loss, air) are recipe-author-vetted per De Paiva 2011. User rides HEAT (drive) only.
